title: Lomatogonium carinthiacum
intro: Lomatogonium carinthiacum, also known as Blue Gentian of the Alps, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and succulent leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Europe, for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lomatogonium carinthiacum include gentiogenin, gentiopicrin, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lomatogonium carinthiacum is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Lomatogonium carinthiacum is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Lomatogonium and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is a perennial herb that produces small, white or purple flowers. The classification of Lomatogonium carinthiacum is further detailed at the species level within the family Gentianaceae. The plant is also known as Carinthian forget-me-not or Alpine forget-me-not in English.

The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may help in soothing skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
